name: The Subjects Speak
description: One of the Lazarus subjects — the oldest one, the one that turned its head toward Stone — has been resonating on the storage crystal frequency. It is trying to reach Sela Arev. Stone wants to take it to the Archive so it can speak directly. The oldest subject has never spoken before.
accept_text: She has been awake for most of the past four days. Not from distress — from documentation. The monitoring equipment is running continuous reads and the readings are more active than anything she has seen in twenty years of watching that chamber. 'The oldest subject.' She shows you the output. 'Eleven weeks ago: still. Standard metabolic indicators, minimal crystal activity, the pattern I've seen for fifteen years.' She turns the page. 'Two weeks after I told them about the choice: the crystal activity begins increasing. Not uniformly — specifically, in the resonance band that Brec identified as the storage crystal frequency.' She turns another page. 'Now: the oldest subject is actively transmitting on that frequency. Low power, but deliberate. A structured pattern, not noise.' She pauses. 'I've been analyzing the pattern for four days. It's not language — it's not Elder notation, it's not anything in the Grid's documented protocol set.' She looks at you. 'It's older. It predates the Foundation Grid. It's the first-attempt storage format.' She closes the folder. 'The oldest subject was integrated before the Grid existed. Before the Foundation Grid, before the consent framework, before the Cascade. The compound reached into something that has been there for much longer than Project Lazarus.' She is very quiet. 'It's trying to reach Sela. Sela was there at the beginning — she was there when the first-attempt crystals were still in use. She would recognize this frequency.' She picks up a portable resonance receiver — something she has clearly built specifically for this. 'I need you to take this to the Archive and hold it while the Archive opens the twelve-channel array. When Sela is on the line, I need the receiver to be there so the oldest subject's transmission can be relayed.' She pauses. 'I am going to stay here with the subject. I want to be in the room when it speaks for the first time.' She looks at you. 'Don't tell me what they say to each other. That conversation belongs to them. But tell me whether it happened. Whether it reached.'
complete_text: The Archive receives the receiver and connects it to the twelve-channel array. 'Sela Arev. Incoming transmission on first-attempt storage frequency.' A pause that is longer than the Archive's usual pauses. Then: 'She recognizes it. She is receiving.' What follows you keep, as Stone asked. What you can say: it lasts forty minutes. In the middle of it the Archive's ambient light shifts — not to a different color, but to a different quality, the way a room's light changes when something significant is happening in it and the significance has its own illumination. At one point the Archive says, quietly, not to you: 'She is laughing.' Then silence again, and the transmission continues. When it ends, the Archive is quiet for a long time. Then: 'Sela asks me to transmit the following to Director Stone: the subject knew her. Not from the compound documentation. From before. The subject was one of the people Sela worked with in the first-attempt period, before the Foundation Grid was built, before the integration protocols existed. Three hundred years in the compound. Three hundred years in the Wastes. And the first thing it wanted to say, when it found the frequency and reached across the distance, was her name.' The Archive pauses. 'Sela says: she has been waiting for this conversation since she encoded the first-attempt frequency into the compound's terminal response layer, three hundred years ago. She encoded it so the subjects could find her when they were ready.' Another pause. 'She says: it took longer than she hoped and less time than she feared. She says: tell Stone that it worked. Tell her that everything she built was aimed at this moment and it worked.' When you carry this back to Stone she does not say anything for a long time. Then she sits down. Then she says, very quietly: 'Good.'
